meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Mastering SaaS Pricing: Scale Your Student Projects Today
content.seo_description: Stop building free apps. Learn how to architect professional SaaS pricing models to monetize your portfolio. Advanced tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Breaking past basic projects requires transitioning from static code to dynamic value-based pricing. By integrating Stripe via Antigravity and using Claude Code for infrastructure, you shift from a 'hobbyist' to a 'SaaS architect,' effectively monetizing your user experience and proving the commercial viability of your technical portfolio.
content.intro:
You’ve spent countless hours in your IDE, shipping CRUD apps that live only on your local machine. The adrenaline of the first commit has faded, replaced by the crushing realization that your portfolio lacks the one thing that separates a student from a professional engineer: real-world commercial viability. You are stuck in the 'free-tier' trap, building features instead of businesses.

It is time to cross the chasm. By architecting a robust pricing engine, you transform your codebase into a revenue-generating asset. This tutorial will guide you through the transition from basic functionality to production-grade SaaS pricing, using modern AI-driven development workflows to ensure your projects are as profitable as they are performant.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Don't just pick numbers. Architect your pricing tiers based on user personas, ensuring each level solves a specific pain point with clear feature gating.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my app's core features. Propose three pricing tiers (Basic, Pro, Enterprise) based on value-based pricing principles, ensuring the conversion path is clear for a SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Map my feature set to three distinct pricing tiers and generate a JSON schema for them.'

steps.title: Configure Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Handle subscription events securely. Webhooks are the heartbeat of your SaaS; they must be idempotent and resilient to failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust Node.js webhook handler for Stripe events. Include error logging, signature verification, and idempotent database updates for subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/api/webhooks/stripe.js --with 'Implement idempotent processing for customer.subscription.updated'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating Middleware
steps.description: Protect your premium features with server-side middleware. Never rely on client-side checks to enforce subscription limits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable middleware function that checks the user's active subscription status against a required permission level before allowing access to a route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Add subscriptionMiddleware.js to protect premium API routes'

steps.title: Architect Usage-Based Metering
steps.description: Move beyond flat rates. Implement usage tracking to charge based on consumption—a favorite for modern B2B SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema for usage-based metering. Include logic for daily resets and billing cycle aggregation for a SaaS app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a migration script for a usage_logs table to track API calls per user'

steps.title: Build the Checkout Flow
steps.description: Simplify the conversion. Use Stripe Checkout to handle PCI compliance while maintaining a seamless UX.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a backend route that generates a Stripe Checkout Session based on the selected pricing tier, including success and cancel URL redirects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/api/billing/checkout.js --with 'Implement Stripe Checkout session creation'

pro_tips: Always implement a grace period logic in your database to prevent immediate service disruption on failed payments.
Use a 'Trial-to-Paid' conversion email sequence triggered by the Stripe API status changes, not just your app logic.
Never hardcode pricing IDs; store them in environment variables or a config service to allow updates without redeploying code.
Design your database schema to support 'Proration' early; changing it after you have 100+ customers is a nightmare.
Use 'Feature Flag' services like LaunchDarkly alongside your pricing tiers to test new features with specific user segments before a full rollout.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on deploying an analytics dashboard to track your MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) and churn rate to iterate on your tiers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to move from 'code-writer' to 'value-creator.' Stop building projects that gather dust and start building businesses that scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
